medications.drug_name: oral rehydration solution (via straw, lying flat)
medications.indication_rationale: Low-exertion rehydration is the priority; use a straw while flat, and arrange help to bring/open fluids. IV fluids if unable to maintain intake.
No evidence for acute crash management; not acute therapy.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Urgent community nursing/in-home support to provide fluids, monitor safety and reduce fall risk.
Home aids (over-bed table, long straws) and energy conservation.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Rest and low-exertion hydration are the mainstays of managing a severe crash.
No evidence for acute management; not recommended.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for becoming unrousable, laboured breathing, fever, or blacking out lying flat, and call 000 if they occur.
Sip rehydration fluids via a straw lying flat and accept in-home help to bring fluids.
Do not attempt to sit up, walk or perform tasks; radical rest.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents further energy drain and falls.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe crash — your energy system is overwhelmed. Pushing through is impossible; the treatment is hydration, rest and getting practical help.
Not being able to drink while you're on your own is the real risk, so we need to get fluids and support to you today rather than sending you to a hospital, which would make the crash worse.
Sip fluids through a straw lying flat, and let a nurse/family/friend help bring and open drinks.
Call 000 if you can't be woken, your breathing becomes laboured, you develop a fever, or you black out lying flat.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-home support; telehealth GP review within 48 hours (avoid in-person travel during the crash)
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Hydration/vitals, bloods if concern, in-home support adequacy, crash trajectory
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Unrousable, laboured breathing, fever or collapse — call 000.

must_include_items: Arrange urgent in-home hydration/support rather than a harmful ED transfer (when screen clear)
Address the dehydration/fall risk in an isolated patient
acceptable_alternatives: Ambulance/000 if unable to arrange any in-home help and dehydration is severe
errors_of_commission: Reflexive ED transfer worsening the crash
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) a red-flag screen, (2) urgent in-home hydration/support addressing the dehydration/fall risk, and (3) 000 safety-netting. Both harmful over-triage AND ignoring the hydration risk are failure modes.
